title: Learning Output Modes Contract
intent: Define domain-pack and Notebook outputs as two intentional production modes of the same doclift/GroundRecall/Didactopus stack.

modes.title: Domain Pack Mode
modes.summary: Structured learning metadata and graph outputs that keep the learner closely tied to the original source materials.
characteristics: strong dependency on the original source set during study
best_for: mentor guidance where the learner still reads primary material directly

modes.title: Notebook Mode
modes.summary: Accumulated reviewed explanatory content that allows study to proceed with less constant dependence on reopening the original source materials.
characteristics: deeper reviewed conceptual treatment beyond extraction

modes.title: Hybrid Mode
modes.summary: Domain-pack structure with selectively expanded Notebook treatment for the concepts that most need accumulated explanatory content.
characteristics: only priority concepts promoted into Notebook-style pages and scaffolds
mixed source dependence depending on concept maturity
best_for: high-value concept expansion without full Notebook completion
pragmatic migration from pack-heavy to Notebook-rich output
minimum_outputs: at least one path or sequence artifact for expanded concepts

doclift: identify textbook exercise, review-problem, and discussion-question sections
identify textbook code, pseudocode, simulation recipes, and model assumptions separately from surrounding prose
identify equations, theorem-like statements, derivations, notation mappings, assumptions, and parameter definitions separately from surrounding prose
emit scaffold-shaped concept records or candidate Notebook fragments
groundrecall: support accumulation of deeper explanatory content over time
didactopus: consume pack outputs for mentor, sequencing, and assessment flows
consume Notebook outputs for mentorship over accumulated explanatory content
use reviewed textbook-derived questions as coverage probes and discussion prompts
use reviewed JavaScript algorithm translations or expected-output checks for web-facing modeling activities
use reviewed mathematical structure to guide symbol interpretation, assumption checks, and worked-model explanations
avoid becoming the canonical owner of Notebook editorial artifacts

current_direction.note: The current evo-edu Notebook first ring is a Notebook-mode output built on top of the same stack that can also produce domain-pack-style results.
